data.title: Nike plans to lay off 775 US distribution employees for automation, adding to 1,000 cuts last summer, affecting TN, MS centers. Macy's also restructuring.
data.description: In a move aimed at boosting profits and increasing automation, Nike Inc. (NYSE: NKE) is set to lay off 775 employees at its U.S. distribution centers, on top of the 1,000 corporate job cuts reported last summer. The majority of the layoffs will primarily impact distribution center roles in Tennessee and Mississippi, where Nike operates its main warehouses. This decision by Nike coincides with a larger trend of restructuring within the retail sector, exemplified by Macy's Inc. (NYSE: M) recent closure of multiple facilities in Connecticut, resulting in over 1,000 job losses. Nike has yet to respond to Benzinga's request for comment on the matter.

data.title: Tineco introduces smarter floor care products for modern living; focuses on performance and design.
data.description: In Paris, Tineco, a prominent provider of smart floor care solutions, is meeting the growing demand for appliances that offer high performance, elegant design, and lasting benefits in modern homes. Through a thoughtfully crafted product selection, Tineco is catering to the desire for enhanced cleaning experiences that align with contemporary living standards. The company's innovative approach merges cutting-edge technology with user-friendly designs in their latest collection, effectively addressing the daily cleaning requirements of households.

data.title: UnitedHealth (NYSE: UNH) faces high medical costs, impacting margins. Q3 saw consolidated medical care ratio at 89.9%.Cost trends project to exceed 11%.
data.description: Health insurer UnitedHealth Group Inc. (NYSE: UNH) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter results soon and investors are closely monitoring the impact of high medical costs on the company's margins and profitability during the past year. In its third-quarter results, UnitedHealth acknowledged that cost pressures in the industry were high and anticipated this trend to continue. The company's consolidated medical care ratio, representing the percentage of premium revenue utilized for medical care, rose to 89.9% from 85.2% year-over-year, driven by increased utilization of physician, outpatient, and inpatient services. UnitedHealth's own forecasts suggest medical cost trends exceeding 11%. For the complete article, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Multiverse acquires StackFuel to offer AI skills to 100,000 German workers, investing in team and product to meet demand.
data.description: A data and AI upskilling provider based in Berlin has been acquired by Multiverse, which is Europe's only EdTech unicorn. The terms of the deal involve a significant investment into StackFuel's team and product to meet the growing demand for upskilling and workforce transformation. Multiverse aims to enhance its AI upskilling programs in response to the need for scalable solutions in the industry.

data.title: Micron investing $24 billion in Singapore for new wafer plant due to AI-driven demand. 1,600 new jobs to be created.
data.description: Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ: MU) is constructing a cutting-edge wafer fabrication plant in Singapore with a whopping investment of around $24 billion (SG$31 billion) over the next decade. The new facility, part of the NAND Center Of Excellence, will be added to Micron's existing campus in Singapore, providing an extra 700,000 square feet of cleanroom space. Wafer production is set to commence in the latter part of 2028. The project is expected to create roughly 1,600 new jobs, supplementing the previously announced 1,400 roles for the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) advanced packaging facility, making the total job creation by Micron in Singapore around 3,000. This expansion is crucial as the demand for memory chips, fueled by AI technology, soars. The construction of the new facility amplifies Micron's efforts to address the ongoing shortage of memory chips.

data.title: China approves Huahui Health's Libevitug injection for treating hepatitis D; first of its kind; NMPA grants conditional approval.
data.description: On January 27, 2026, Huahui Health revealed that the China National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) has provided conditional approval for its Libevitug injection to address chronic hepatitis D virus infection in adults, with or without compensated cirrhosis.
